title: calacinum platycladum
intro: Calacinum platycladum, also known as calumba or jack-in-the-bush, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Menispermaceae. Calumba has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and anti-diabetic effects, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and treat various gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of calumba include its potential to help manage blood sugar levels, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of stomach ulcers and diarrhea. Calumba can be prepared as a decoction, powder, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to support digestive health and address various metabolic imbalances.
